leagueBriefing: Brentford's European dreams took a slight dent as they allowed bottom-of-the-table Wolves to claw back from two goals down in a thrilling Monday night draw.

games.headline: Arokodare caps Wolves comeback as Brentford let lead slip
games.brief: Brentford looked to be cruising toward the European spots after a dominant opening 40 minutes, but a resilient Wolverhampton Wanderers side refused to stay down. Igor Thiago celebrated his maiden Brazil call-up with a goal that seemed to have the Bees in complete control at 2-0. However, Adam Armstrong's stunner just before the break changed the mood entirely, allowing the visitors to find a second-half equalizer through substitute Tolu Arokodare and maintain their recent unbeaten spark.
bullets: Igor Thiago marked his first-ever call-up to the Brazil national team with his 19th league goal of the season.
Michael Kayode opened the scoring with a powerful header, marking his first goal in 41 appearances for the Bees.
Adam Armstrong ignited the comeback with a sublime 25-yard strike into the top corner just before halftime.
Substitute Tolu Arokodare equalized with a stooping header only three minutes after coming off the bench.
Wolves struck the woodwork twice in the second half through Armstrong and Arokodare, nearly snatching all three points.
games.context: The draw leaves Wolves 12 points from safety while Brentford remain in seventh, missed a chance to move within a point of the top six.

highlights.title: Brentford v. Wolves | PREMIER LEAGUE HIGHLIGHTS | 3/16/2026 | NBC Sports
